parsed_review.summary: Read-only review of CP00-498 (RP16.P05.M06.S11-S20, risk A, 10 units, micro "Synthetic Fixture Set") performed via Read/Grep/Glob only. The pack is descriptor-only and its pack-level claim is correctly scoped: manifest reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, production_ready_flag="governance_core_p05_synthetic_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified" (a descriptor-verification flag, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim). No production_ready gate is weakened. Consistency is verified end-to-end: closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-498 (10 units; deliverables security_audit:2/implementation:3/fixture:1/test:3/hermes_evidence:1) == baseline == contract cp498_requirements == registry GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP498_REQUIREMENTS == manifest distribution. The contract's current_pack, cp498_requirements, cp498_no_write_attestation, p05_synthetic_fixture_slice_descriptor, and trailing historical_pack_binding all pin CP00-498 (upstream CP00-497, next CP00-499, next_subphase RP16.P05.M06.S21). The validator (scripts/validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs) enforces deepEqual between the contract-embedded descriptor and the code-generated descriptor, asserts CP00-498 exists in the plan, and checks coverage/slice/handoff; model.test.js loads the real contract and plan and adds 3 CP00-498 tests covering coverage, descriptor-only rows, and evidence/handoff authority boundaries. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are present and enforced: all rows descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, raw_payload_included=false; freezeCp498Result + no_write_attestation set every dispatch/write/audit/storage/hermes-runtime/permission flag false and synthetic_only/no_real_data true; no real client/matter/secret data and no payloads appear in fixtures. Claude remains a read-only, non-final approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob), and Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; H16 evidence object), not product runtime. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: readiness auth_preflight produced valid non-empty JSON (exit 0, 1436 bytes, is_error=false), command-evidence shows all required validation commands exit 0, no prior invalid review attempts recorded. One P3 informational observation on the rolling runtime-opening deferral; no P0/P1/P2. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected at the read_only_claude_run stage and produced downstream before commit. I am not the final approver; human approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim, which this pack correctly does not assert from local validation.

findings.title: Runtime-opening target continues to roll forward (CP00-497 named CP00-498; CP00-498 now defers to CP00-499)
findings.details: CP00-497's authority_boundary.runtime_opening_pack_id was 'CP00-498', implying CP00-498 would open governance runtime. CP00-498's authority_boundary.runtime_opening_pack_id is now 'CP00-499' (contract line 18653; set in code as next_pack_id at packages/governance/src/service.js:2394). This is structurally a rolling pointer to the next pack rather than a firm commitment, and the descriptor-only build-up across RP16 governance (CP00-480..CP00-498) has not yet opened any runtime surface. This is not a defect in CP00-498 — the pack honestly reports production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / descriptor_only=true and makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim — but an independent reviewer flags it so the human final approver does not assume governance runtime has been exercised or validated.
findings.recommendation: No source change required for CP00-498. The human final approver should track the deferral pattern when assessing overall enterprise-trust progress and confirm a downstream pack is explicitly scoped to open and validate governance runtime rather than the pointer perpetually advancing.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational, non-blocking. Confirmed consistent with the pack's descriptor-only production_ready=false posture and the standing descriptor-first build approach. Does not block CP00-498 pack closeout or goal closeout; no P0/P1 implications.
